title: Donald Trump Sends Out Embarrassing New Year's Eve Message; This is Disturbing
text: Donald Trump just couldn't wish all Americans a Happy New Year and leave it at that. Instead, the article says he gave a shout out to his enemies, haters, and the fake news media. The piece frames his holiday message as petty and unpresidential and includes reactions from critics on social media.

title: Drunk Bragging Trump Staffer Started Russian Collusion Investigation
text: The article says George Papadopoulos revealed information about Russian opposition research on Hillary Clinton while drunk in a wine bar. It argues this, rather than the Steele dossier, helped trigger the Russia investigation. The piece presents the Trump presidency as chaotic and compares it to a badly scripted reality TV show.

title: Sheriff David Clarke Becomes An Internet Joke For Threatening To Poke People In The Eye
text: This article claims former Milwaukee Sheriff David Clarke responded aggressively to reports about an FBI search warrant tied to his communications. It quotes inflammatory social media posts and emphasizes mocking online reactions. The framing is overtly hostile and rhetorical.

title: As U.S. budget fight looms, Republicans flip their fiscal script
text: Washington lawmakers were preparing for a budget fight in early 2018. The report says Republicans pushed tax cuts that would increase debt while also calling for spending restraint. It describes tensions over military spending, domestic programs, immigration, and disaster aid.

title: U.S. military to accept transgender recruits on Monday: Pentagon
text: The Pentagon said transgender people would be allowed to enlist starting on January 1 after court rulings blocked the administration's ban. The report explains that the Justice Department chose not to appeal those rulings at that stage. It also summarizes the broader legal and political dispute over the policy.

title: Senior U.S. Republican senator: Let Mr. Mueller do his job
text: Senator Lindsey Graham said the special counsel investigation into Russia and the 2016 election should continue without political interference. The article notes that Trump allies had criticized Mueller, while multiple congressional committees and the special counsel were still investigating. It also mentions questions around the Steele dossier and George Papadopoulos.
